title: The Long-Tailed Distribution of Real-World Data
description: A frequency graph showing the prevalence of common events (head) versus the vast number of rare events (tail) in a typical real-world dataset, illustrating why standard models can fail and the need for specialized techniques.
elements: Decision Boundary of a Standard Model
relationships: The Head contains a few frequent events that standard models learn well
The Tail contains a vast number of unique, rare events that standard models often misclassify or ignore
The Decision Boundary is optimized for the Head, leading to poor performance on the Tail
